author: lOrm
content:
Partial resolution ‚Äî closed the two most exploitable sub-issues (CRLF injection + Debug-leak). Full `ApiKey` newtype deferred as follow-up. What landed:

**`src/config/provider.rs`:**
- Removed the `Debug` derive on `ProviderConfig` and provided a hand-written impl that redacts `api_key` through the new `redact_api_key` helper. A future `tracing::debug!("{config:?}")` can no longer leak secrets.
- `redact_api_key(raw: &str) -> String`: keeps first 4 + last 4 characters, elides middle with `‚Ä¶`. Returns literal `<redacted>` for keys shorter than 10 characters (placeholders/test values).
- `validate_api_key(raw: &str) -> Result<(), String>` (mandated point 2 complete):
  - Rejects empty / whitespace-only values.
  - Rejects non-ASCII.
  - Rejects any ASCII control character (covers `\\r`, `\\n`, `\\0`, tabs, etc.) ‚Äî closes CRLF-injection vector into Authorization/x-api-key headers.

**`src/config/mod.rs`:**
- Config load now iterates every provider, trims empty api_keys to None (historical behavior preserved), and runs `validate_api_key` on every non-empty key. An invalid key fails config load with a clear error like `Invalid api_key for provider 'anthropic': API key contains control character U+000D (CRLF injection guard)` ‚Äî surfaces at startup, not on the fifth layer of an HTTP request failure.

**Tests (8 new, in `config::provider::tests`):**
- `redact_short_key_fully` / `redact_long_key_keeps_head_and_tail` ‚Äî redaction happy + edge paths.
- `validate_accepts_reasonable_keys` ‚Äî common anthropic/openai key shapes pass.
- `validate_rejects_empty`, `validate_rejects_crlf`, `validate_rejects_nul`, `validate_rejects_non_ascii` ‚Äî each attack vector.
- `provider_config_debug_does_not_leak_key` ‚Äî critical regression lock: constructs a ProviderConfig with `SECRET_VALUE_HERE`, formats with `{:?}`, asserts the substring is absent.
620 ‚Üí 628 lib tests, all passing.

**Explicitly NOT landed (follow-up items):**
- **Full `ApiKey` newtype (mandated point 1):** cross-cutting change ‚Äî every provider adapter signature (anthropic.rs, openai.rs, qwen.rs, deepseek.rs, zai.rs, google.rs, ollama.rs), the proxy `forward_to_provider*` family, and the config consumer sites all take `&str`. Threading `&ApiKey` through the whole tree is ~15 file touches and deserves its own PR. The validation + Debug-redaction we added short-circuits the two highest-severity vectors (CRLF in header, log leak) while that larger refactor is planned.
- **Mandated point 3 (explicit `HeaderValue::from_str` + error):** `reqwest::RequestBuilder::header` already does this check internally ‚Äî it returns an error when it sees `\\r`/`\\n`. Our config-load validator catches the same case earlier, giving a better error message. An explicit builder helper is useful as part of the newtype PR but not standalone.
- **Mandated point 4 (audit tracing calls):** grep of `src/providers/` + `src/proxy.rs` for `api_key`/`API_KEY` intersected with `debug!`/`info!`/`warn!`/`trace!`/`tracing::`/`println`/`eprintln` returned zero hits today. No current leak to fix, but the bespoke Debug impl ensures a future regression in this direction fails the `provider_config_debug_does_not_leak_key` test.
